    Alerts & notifications
    Critical — act now
    DATP Public Comment reading room is live. The City of Colorado Springs Digital Accessibility Transition Plan (Draft 2026), with the full plan embedded as a PDF and twelve structured public comments residents can adapt and submit. Comment portal links live in the reading room.
    Public comment · COS Digital Accessibility Transition Plan · May 2026
    Open the DATP reading room →
    BOCC petition — service plan transparency & algorithmic pricing disclosure. A general resident petition with four narrow asks within existing BOCC authority. Sign and send.
    Civic action · El Paso County BOCC · May 2026
    Open petition letter →
    Veterans' petition to the EPC BOCC. Companion petition for veterans of any branch — service plan transparency and algorithmic pricing protections, framed around military housing harms.
    Civic action · Veterans · El Paso County BOCC · May 2026
    Open veterans' petition →
    LDC update — public comment on service plan transparency. A 300-word comment ready to submit through the El Paso County Land Development Code update process.
    Public comment · LDC update · El Paso County · May 2026
    Open LDC comment →
    Background brief — algorithmic rent pricing in El Paso County. Sourced reference for journalists, policy analysts, and the COS Chamber housing committee: federal antitrust action, local policy options, and the case for BOCC-level service plan transparency.
    Reference · Housing & pricing · El Paso County BOCC · May 2026
    Read background brief →
    HOME-ARP comment window is live — closes May 26, 2026. COS is collecting public comment on the HOME-ARP Allocation Plan amendment via an ArcGIS Survey123 form Esri's own ACR rates as "Partially Supports" on WCAG 2.1 A/AA. A WCAG 2.1 AA Drupal Webform replacement is ready to install — bilingual EN/ES, JSON webhook + staff email + native storage, inline accommodation intake.
    24 CFR 91.105 · ADA Title II · May 4, 2026
    Open the accessible HTML form preview → Open the HOME-ARP bundle README →
    Downtown Funding and Records Search Search BID assessments, DDA grants, TIF reimbursements, board meetings, and financial records in one place.
    Annual adopted budget — C.R.S. 29-1-101 et seq · April 10, 2026
    Open the search portal →
    ADA Title II deadline: April 24, 2026 — 16 days. All local government websites must meet WCAG 2.1 AA. Zero district sites are confirmed compliant on PDF documents.
    HB21-1110 · 28 C.F.R. Part 35 · April 8, 2026
    Open district audit dashboard →
    249 of 284 metro districts have no website. CORA request is the only way a resident can access their own district's records.
    EPC Assessor audit · April 2026
    See district audit →
    WS Districts (WSDM) serves 30+ districts on a single WordPress site. Last calendar entry: 2021. No governance docs. No financials. One compliance letter fixes all 30+ simultaneously.
    Platform audit · April 2026
    Draft compliance letter →
    High priority
    All posted PDFs likely fail WCAG 1.3.1. Meeting minutes, budgets, and audit docs are almost certainly untagged. This is the real HB21-1110 failure — not the homepage.
    ADA document audit · April 2026
    View ADA analysis →
    0 of 8 audited sites post their service plan. C.R.S. §32-1-202 requires it. This is the foundational legal document. Not one district links it publicly.
    Governance gap · §32-1-202
    See audit dashboard →
    Annual audit documents: publicly posted by 0 of 8 districts. Required by §32-1-106. Due to State Auditor by July 31. The clock is running.
    Governance gap · §32-1-106 · due July 31
    See audit dashboard →
    Informational
    2 CORA requests pending response. Military housing numbers and district governance records both awaiting reply. Response window is still open.
    CORA tracker · April 2026
    View tracker →
    Wolfersberger compliance and Bellamese analysis complete. New findings ready for review on developer agreements, compliance history, and parcel-level records.
    Compliance analysis · April 2026
    Read analysis →
    Jurisdiction finder is live. Enter any El Paso County address to find your metro districts, school district, city jurisdiction, and utilities — plain language, no login.
    New · April 2026
    Find my jurisdiction →
    CSPD Blotter — Weekly History is live. 271 incidents pulled directly from the live blotter feed, paired with a Poisson 95% confidence band to flag statistical anomalies week to week. Tabs are full APG-pattern keyboard accessible; every chart has an aria-label.
    New · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
    Open blotter dashboard →
    Lorson Ranch financial dashboard is live. Seven cross-pledged metro districts, $94.2M aggregate audited deficit, IGA money flows, and a per-home tax calculator — sourced from 2026 budgets and 2024 audits. Companion plain-language briefing available as a download.
    New · Special Districts · April 2026
    Open Lorson Ranch dashboard →
    CBI Missing Persons form — accessible rebuild is live. Resolves all seven critical/serious findings from the ADA audit: skip link, live regions, focus management, EN/ES localization, real-time validation messaging. Drops into the existing Drupal Webform infrastructure with optional Claude API routing.
    New · State · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
    Open CBI form rebuild →
    LexisNexis ADAM Program dashboard — accessible rebuild is live. Audit and reference rebuild of the missing-children dashboard: APG-pattern tabs with roving tabindex and full keyboard nav, solid amber focus rings (3:1 against any surface), darker success-green for AA contrast, and "View Poster" placeholders converted from dead anchors to real buttons.
    New · Private Sector · April 2026 · WCAG 2.1 AA
    Open ADAM dashboard →
    ADA deadline · April 24 · 16 days 284 metro districts · El Paso County 249 districts · zero web presence 64,434 crash records · COS 2017–2025 390 fatalities over 9 years No invoice attached 0% of districts post service plans publicly Built by residents · Colorado Springs ADA deadline · April 24 · 16 days 284 metro districts · El Paso County 249 districts · zero web presence 64,434 crash records · COS 2017–2025 390 fatalities over 9 years No invoice attached 0% of districts post service plans publicly Built by residents · Colorado Springs

    StorySeed Studios · The Dais · El Paso County, Colorado

    Trying to figure
    things out —
    one step at a time.

    284 metro districts. One county. Zero invoices attached. Everything here is free, built from public records, and written so that a resident can read it — not just a lawyer or a lobbyist.

    Search the site

    Type a topic, an agency, a record, or an address. Filter by jurisdiction tier. Address lookups route to the finder.

    Filtering happens as you type. Press Enter to deep-link the search.

      No matches in this tier. Try a different filter or a shorter query.

      Stay informed

      Get email updates from your government — your way.

      Pick the offices, dashboards, and districts that matter to you. Choose how often we email (immediate, daily, weekly, or monthly digest). Tell us what else you wish we covered.

      Choose your topics →
      You

      Start with where you are

      Jurisdiction → district → everything else

      Because if you don't know where you are on the map, nothing else on this site quite makes sense yet.

      If you don't know where you are on the map, how do you know what's going on?

      El Paso County has 284 metro districts. You're probably in at least one — and paying a mill levy for it on every property tax bill. But which district? Who manages it? What do they owe you, legally? It starts with your address.

      Routes to the jurisdiction finder with your input. Results will load immediately — no typing again.

      Districts in EPC
      284
      Confirmed via Assessor
      Have a website
      ~35
      12% of all districts
      Dark — no web access
      249
      CORA request only
      ADA deadline
      Apr 24
      16 days from today
      Dashboards

      Everything, grouped by jurisdiction

      Each card holds one or more pages

      Each dashboard lives under the jurisdiction whose records it covers. Cards with multiple links mean that scope has a small family of pages that belong together.

      Tier 1 · Where you live

      Local

      El Paso County & Colorado Springs

      Find what governs your address. BOCC petitions, CSU water data, CSPD records, sheriff dashboard, downtown funding, the 284 metro districts billed to your property — everything that touches your address day to day.

      Browse Local →

      Tier 2 · Colorado

      State

      Statewide agencies reaching El Paso County

      CDPHE health data, CBI missing persons, CCRD complaint paths, DOLA's special-district registry under CRS Title 32, and the Colorado Attorney General's enforcement surface.

      Browse State →

      Tier 3 · Federal & private sector

      National

      Federal agencies setting the floor

      DOJ Civil Rights and antitrust, FTC consumer protection, FBI intake, HUD funding behind HOME-ARP, and private-sector platforms operated at national scale.

      Browse National →

      Want the structural overview instead? See the full government org chart →

      People

      Who you need to know

      BOCC · District managers · State contacts

      The organizations and offices that make decisions affecting your property, your taxes, and your streets — whether you know their names or not.

      EPC
      El Paso County BOCC
      Board of County Commissioners · 5 members · Oversight of all metro districts in unincorporated EPC
      Decision maker
      WSD
      WS Districts (WSDM)
      Manages 30+ metro district websites · Single WordPress platform · Primary compliance contact for bulk remediation
      Compliance target
      SG
      Streamline Government
      Platform for 4 district sites · 13 districts covered · Best ADA baseline · Contact for PDF tagging requirements
      Platform vendor
      DOJ
      U.S. DOJ Civil Rights Division
      ADA Title II enforcement · 28 C.F.R. Part 35 · Complaint intake for districts failing April 24 deadline
      Federal enforcement
      OSA
      Colorado State Auditor
      Receives annual district audits by July 31 · C.R.S. §32-1-106 · Currently receives what the public cannot access
      State oversight
      DL
      DOLA — Dept. of Local Affairs
      Colorado's municipal oversight body · Metro district registration and service plan approval · CORA contact
      State oversight
      Full contact lists in the citizen toolkit
      Open citizen toolkit — all contacts →
      Events

      Dates that matter

      Deadlines · meetings · filings

      The dates that move slowly until they don't. A few of these are legally binding. Most residents have never heard of them.

      Apr
      24
      ADA Title II compliance deadline — all local government websites
      WCAG 2.1 AA required for all public entity websites under 28 C.F.R. Part 35. HB21-1110 applies to PDF documents. Zero district sites confirmed compliant on document accessibility. 16 days.
      Critical · 16 days
      Jul
      31
      Annual audit due to Colorado State Auditor — all metro districts
      C.R.S. §32-1-106 requires annual audits submitted to the State Auditor by July 31. Currently, 0 of 8 audited districts post these publicly. The State Auditor sees it. Residents don't.
      High · Q3
      May
      6
      El Paso County BOCC regular meeting
      BOCC meetings are the primary venue for metro district oversight action. Draft BOCC resolution requiring service plans and audit docs on all district websites — full template in the citizen toolkit.
      Meeting
      May
      20
      CORA response window closes — pending requests
      Two outstanding CORA requests (military housing numbers, district governance records). Colorado law requires response within 3 business days with up to 7-day extension. Response window tracked in CORA tracker.
      CORA deadline
      Jan
      31
      BOCC proposed mandate — tagged PDFs on all district sites
      Proposed: BOCC resolution requiring all publicly posted PDFs to be WCAG 1.3.1 tagged by January 31, 2027. Currently zero sites comply. One resolution, 284 districts. Proposed — not yet filed.
      Proposed '27
      Coming up

      What's in the queue

      No ETA. We have day jobs.

      The list is longer than this page. That's intentional.

      County Dashboard
      El Paso County — all jurisdictions, all crash data, full picture.

      LOSS Corridor Ratings
      Danger ratings for every major road. Pending records request filed April 2026.

      Zip Code Search
      Your block, your crashes, your street. Hyper-local lookup coming.

      Metro District Portal
      Unified public interface for the 249 districts with no website. CORA shouldn't be the only option.

      State Dashboard
      CDOT data across all 64 counties. Colorado from 30,000 feet.

      Mill Levy Explainer
      You're paying it. Every property tax bill. No district explains what it is or why.

      Bond & Debt Tracker
      Outstanding balances, maturity dates, resident liability. The stuff that outlasts administrations.

      Stories
      Long-form analysis when the data says something nobody's saying out loud.

      A lot more
      The list is longer than this page. That's intentional.

      What is this, exactly?

      cheetochopsticks is a civic data project built by two residents of Colorado Springs — StorySeed Studios and The Dais — at a kitchen table, with public records, too much coffee, and a sincere belief that people who live somewhere deserve to actually understand it.

      Everything here is free. No paywall, no pitch deck, no monetization strategy. No invoice attached. If it helps one person ask a better question at a city council meeting, or understand why their street feels dangerous, or find out which metro district is charging them a mill levy they can't look up — that's the whole point.

      The Alerts button in the nav is real. Those nine notifications are current. If you want to know when something new drops — before the press release, before the tweet — bookmark this page and check the ticker. We'll be here.

      Get email updates

      We'll email you a plain-language summary with links to the source documents whenever this office posts new public-record material.

      By subscribing you agree to receive emails about this topic. We'll send a confirmation link first. Unsubscribe any time. (consent v2026-04-30-v1)
